name: bosdyn-core

metadata.name: bosdyn-core
metadata.summary: Boston Dynamics API Core code and interfaces

documentation:
# bosdyn-core

The bosdyn-core wheel contains core helper functions for the Boston Dynamics Spot API. The classes defined in this wheel are used by the clients in [bosdyn-client](https://pypi.org/project/bosdyn-client/) and [bosdyn-mission](https://pypi.org/project/bosdyn-mission/) wheels to communicate with the services running on the Spot robots. The wheel contains two classes: - **Geometry**: Helper functions to convert between quaternions and Euler XYZ orientations. - **Util**: Common utility functions for API Python code.

A class for registering a series which stores protobuf messages in a message series.

The series is named by a 'channel_name' which defaults to the full type name of the

documentation:
Add the given series_descriptor to the index, with the given file offset.

Args:

 series_block_file_offset  Location in file where SeriesDescriptor will be written, or
                            was read from.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.file_indexer.FileIndexer.add_series
documentation:
Register a new series for messages for a DataWriter.

Args:
 series_type:   the kind of spec, corresponding to the set of keys expected in series_spec.
 series_spec:   dict of {key (string) -> value (string)} describing the series.
 message_type:  MessageTypeDescriptor (need EITHER this OR pod_type)
 pod_type:      PodTypeDescriptor (need EITHER this OR pod_type)
 annotations:   optional dict of key (string) -> value (string) pairs to
                    associate with the message channel
 additional_index_names: names of additional timestamps to store with
                                each message (list of string).
 writer:        BlockWriter owned by the DataWriter.

Returns series id (int).

Raises SeriesNotUniqueError if a series matching series_spec is already added.

An object which keeps an index of series and blocks within series.

It can write a block index at the end of a data file.

modules.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer

classes.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.__init__
documentation:
Args:
 outfile:       a file-like objet for writing binary data (e.g., from open(fname, 'wb')).
 annotations:   optional dict of key (string) -> value (string) pairs.
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.file_index
documentation: Get the FileIndex proto used which describes how to access data in the file.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.add_message_series
documentation:
Add a new series for storing message data.  Message data is variable-sized binary data.

Args:
 series_type:   the kind of spec, corresponding to the set of keys expected in series_spec.
 series_spec:   dict of {key (string) -> value (string)} describing the series.
 content_type:  data encoding, like http content-type header (string)
 type_name:     string describing the kind of data
 is_metadata:   Metadata messages are needed to interpret other messages which
                 may be stored in the file.  If the file is split into parts,
                 metadata messages must be duplicated into each part. (default=False)
 annotations:   optional dict of key (string) -> value (string) pairs to
                  associate with the message channel
 additional_index_names: names of additional timestamps to store with
                                each message (list of string).

Returns series id (int).

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.add_pod_series
documentation:
Add a new series for storing data POD data (float, double, int, etc....).

Args:
 series_type:   the kind of spec, corresponding to the set of keys expected in series_spec.
 series_spec:   dict of {key (string) -> value (string)} describing the series.
 type_enum:     kind of values stored in the file (PodTypeEnum).
 dimension:     None or empty-array means elements are single values,
                   [3] means vectors of size 3, [4, 4] is a 4x4 matrix, etc....
 annotations:   optional dict of key (string) -> value (string) pairs to
                    associate with the message channel

Returns series id (int).

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.add_series
documentation:
Register a new series for messages.

Args:
 series_type:   the kind of spec, corresponding to the set of keys expected in series_spec.
 series_spec:   dict of {key (string) -> value (string)} describing the series.
 message_type:  MessageTypeDescriptor (need EITHER this OR pod_type)
 pod_type:      PodTypeDescriptor (need EITHER this OR pod_type)
 annotations:   optional dict of key (string) -> value (string) pairs to
                    associate with the message channel
 additional_index_names: names of additional timestamps to store with
                                each message (list of string).

Returns series id (int).

Raises SeriesNotUniqueError if a series matching series_spec is already added.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.write_data
documentation:
Store binary data into the file, under a previously-defined channel.

Args:
 series_index:   integer returned when series was registered with the file.
 timestamp_nsec: nsec since unix epoch to timestamp the data.
 data:           binary data to store.
 additional_indexes: additional timestamps if needed for this channel.

Raises:
    DataFormatError if the data or additional_indexes are not valid for this series.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.data_writer.DataWriter.run_on_close
documentation: Register a function to be called when file is closed, before index is written.
Class for writing data to a file.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.pod_series_writer.PodSeriesWriter.write
documentation:
Add sample to data block, and write block if block is full.

A block is full if there is no room for an additional sample within the data_block_size.

Args:
 timestamp_nsec:  nsec since unix epoch to timestamp the data
 sample:          array/vector of POD values to write

Raises DataFormatError if the data is invalid for this series.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.pod_series_writer.PodSeriesWriter.finish_block
documentation: If there are samples which haven't been written to the file, write them now.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader.version
documentation: Return file version as a bosdyn.api.FileFormatVersion proto.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader.annotations
documentation: Return {key -> value} dict for file annotations.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader.file_index
documentation: Get the FileIndex proto used which describes how to access data in the file.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader.checksum
documentation: 160-bit checksum read from the end of the file, or None if not yet read.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader.read_checksum
documentation: Override to compute checksum on reading, in stream-readers.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader._computed_checksum
documentation: Override to compute checksum on reading, in stream-readers.

methods.name: bosdyn.bddf.base_data_reader.BaseDataReader.series_spec_to_index
documentation:
Given a series spec (map {key -> value}), return the series index for that series.

Raises ValueError if no such series exists.
Shared parent class for DataReader and StreamedDataReader.

functions.name: bosdyn.deprecated.moved_to
documentation: Deprecate a function because it was moved to another location.

functions.name: bosdyn.deprecated.renamed_to
documentation: Deprecate a function because it was renamed.

functions.name: bosdyn.deprecated._deprecate_to_new
documentation:
Deprecation helper that creates a wrapper with a new docstring that deprecated() can modify.
When using deprecated.sphinx.deprecated, it modifies the docstring of the wrapped function.
For cases where we have moved a function and want to deprecate the old location, we would often
write

    old_name = deprecated(reason = ...)(new_name)

which has the unfortunate side effect of modifying the new name to say in its docstring that it
is deprecated.

This alternate version will prevent the original's docstring from being modified, only modifying
the wrapper's docstring.

classes.name: bosdyn.geometry.EulerZXY
documentation:
Orientation represented by Yaw('Z')-Roll('X')-Pitch('Y') order Euler angles.
Each angle is expressed in radians.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.set_clock_source
documentation: Set the clock source to use the input clock source.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.duration_str
documentation:
Return a formatted string for a duration proto.

Args:
  duration (google.protobuf.Duration): input duration

Returns:
  string: format '{seconds}.{nanoseconds}'.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.duration_to_seconds
documentation:
Return a number of seconds, as a float, based on Duration protobuf fields.

If you want a precise duration, use the Duration class's ToTimedelta function.

Args:
  duration (google.protobuf.Duration): input duration

functions.name: bosdyn.util.seconds_to_duration
documentation:
Return a protobuf Duration from number of seconds, as a float.

Args:
  seconds (float): duration length

functions.name: bosdyn.util.seconds_to_timestamp
documentation:
Return a protobuf Timestamps from number of seconds, as a float.

Args:
  seconds (float): seconds since epoch

functions.name: bosdyn.util.timestamp_str
documentation:
Return a formatted string for a timestamp or a duration proto.

Args:
  timestamp (google.protobuf.Timestamp or google.protobuf.Duration): input duration

Returns:
  A string with format '{seconds}.{nanoseconds}'.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.sec_to_nsec
documentation:
Convert time in seconds as from _clock_source_fn() to a timestamp in nanoseconds.

Args:
 secs: Time in seconds
Returns:
 The time in nanoseconds, as an integer.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.nsec_to_sec
documentation:
Convert time in nanoseconds to a timestamp in seconds.

Args:
 secs: Time in nanoseconds
Returns:
 The time in seconds, as a float.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.now_nsec
documentation: Returns nanoseconds from dawn of unix epoch until when this is called.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.now_sec
documentation: Returns seconds from dawn of unix epoch until when this is called.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.set_timestamp_from_now
documentation:
Sets google.protobuf.Timestamp to point to the current time on the system clock.

Args:
 timestamp_proto[out] (google.protobuf.Timestamp): The proto into which the time will be written

functions.name: bosdyn.util.now_timestamp
documentation: Returns a google.protobuf.Timestamp set to the current time on the system clock.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.set_timestamp_from_nsec
documentation:
Sets a Timestamp protobuf from an integer of nanoseconds since the unix epoch.

Args:
 timestamp_proto[out] (google.protobuf.Timestamp):  timestamp into which time will be written
 time_nsec[in]:         the time, as an integer of nanoseconds from the unix epoch

functions.name: bosdyn.util.set_timestamp_from_datetime
documentation:
Sets a Timestamp protobuf from datetime.datetime value.

Args:
 timestamp_proto[out] (google.protobuf.Timestamp):  timestamp into which time will be written
 date_time[in] (datetime.datetime)                  the time to convert

functions.name: bosdyn.util.nsec_to_timestamp
documentation:
Returns a google.protobuf.Timestamp for an integer value of nanoseconds since the unix epoch.

Args:
 time_nsec (int): the time, as an integer of nanoseconds from the unix epoch

functions.name: bosdyn.util.timestamp_to_sec
documentation:
From a Timestamp proto, return a floating point value of seconds from the unix epoch .

Args:
 timestamp_proto (google.protobuf.Timestamp): input time

functions.name: bosdyn.util.timestamp_to_nsec
documentation:
From a Timestamp proto, return an integer of nanoseconds from the unix epoch.

Args:
 timestamp_proto (google.protobuf.Timestamp):  input time

functions.name: bosdyn.util.timestamp_to_datetime
documentation:
Convert a google.protobuf.Timestamp to a Python datetime.datetime object.

Args:
 timestamp_proto (google.protobuf.Timestamp): input time
 use_nanos (bool):        use fractional seconds in proto (default=True)
Returns:
 a datetime.datetime proto

functions.name: bosdyn.util.secs_to_hms
documentation:
Format a time in seconds as 'H:MM:SS'

Args:
 seconds:   number of seconds (will be truncated to integer value)

functions.name: bosdyn.util.distance_str
documentation:
Convert a distance in meters to either xxx.xx m or xxx.xx km

Args:
  meters (float/int):   distance in meters

functions.name: bosdyn.util.format_metric
documentation:
Returns a string representing a metric.

Args:
 metric (bosdyn.api.Parameter):   metric description

functions.name: bosdyn.util.parse_datetime
documentation:
Parse datetime from string

Args:
 val: string with format like as described by TIME_FORMAT_DESC.

Returns: datetime.datetime.

Raises: DatetimeParseError if format of val is not recognized.

functions.name: bosdyn.util.parse_timespan
documentation:
Parse a timespan spec of the form {from-time}[-{to-time}]

Args:
 timespan_spec: string with format {spec} or {spec}-{spec} where {spec} is a string
         with a format as described by TIME_FORMAT_DESC.

Returns: (datetime.datetime, None) or (datetime.datetime, datetime.datetime).

Raises: DatetimeParseError if format of val is not recognized.

classes.name: bosdyn.util.DatetimeParseError
documentation: Failed to parse any datetime formats known to parse_datetime()

classes.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter

methods.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter.robot_timestamp_from_local_nsecs
documentation:
Returns a robot-clock Timestamp proto for a local time in nanoseconds.

Args:
  local_time_nsecs:  Local system time, in integer of nanoseconds from the unix epoch.

methods.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter.robot_timestamp_from_local_secs
documentation:
Returns a robot-clock Timestamp proto for a local time in seconds.

Args:
  local_time_secs:  Local system time, in seconds from the unix epoch.

methods.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter.robot_timestamp_from_local
documentation:
Takes a Timestamp proto is local time and returns one in robot time.

Args:
  local_timestamp_proto (google.protobuf.Timestamp): timestamp in system clock

methods.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter.convert_timestamp_from_local_to_robot
documentation:
Edits timestamp_proto in place to convert it from the local clock to the robot_clock.

Args:
  timestamp_proto[in/out] (google.protobuf.Timestamp): local system time

methods.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter.robot_seconds_from_local_seconds
documentation:
Returns the robot time in seconds from a local time in seconds.

Args:
  local_time_secs:  Local system time, in seconds from the unix epoch.

methods.name: bosdyn.util.RobotTimeConverter.local_seconds_from_robot_timestamp
documentation:
Returns the local time in seconds from a robot-clock Timestamp proto.

Args:
  local_time_secs:  Local system time, in seconds from the unix epoch.

Converts times in the local system clock to times in the robot clock.

Conversions are made given an estimate of clock skew from the local clock to the robot clock.
